<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I understand the request.

The skill targets `content/english/blog/alex-eala-pro-career-tracker.md`. It should update WTA rankings, tournament results, Grand Slam main draw outcomes, and match records against top players.

Matches to Watch Out For will be reorganized by moving opponents who have already played into their relevant sections. The skill will pull data from the WTA Profile, Tennis.com Player Activity, TNNSLIVE, and WTA Head-to-Head URLs. Content formatting conventions from the existing page also need to be followed.

Specific formatting conventions are now clear. Tournament results must be grouped by year with the newest year first. Each entry uses bold tournament and level labels with round names, linked tennis.com and wtatennis.com URLs, and YouTube shortcodes. Head-to-head sections require WTA profiles, H2H links, a Grand Slam titles table, and match lists. Historical milestones appear as sub-bullets. The skill will use Alex Eala’s WTA ID 330332 and competitor ID 627278 as constants.

The skill will be split into two files: `SKILL.md` for the main workflow and `reference.md` for section templates, player ID lists, and formatting rules. I have also catalogued the WTA player IDs for the top opponents already featured on the page.

Additional opponent IDs were added for Emma Raducanu, Sofia Kenin, Elena Rybakina, and Mirra Andreeva. The skill will be placed in `.cursor/skills/update-alex-eala-tracker/`. It will trigger on requests to update the career page, fetch fresh data from authoritative sources, and follow a strict section-checking order. Existing formatting conventions will be preserved. Rules will distinguish between adding new head-to-head sections and updating existing ones. YouTube embeds will only be added if official highlights are provided or found.</details></think>
